title: A Survey on Efficient Inference for Large Language Models
preview: Wide-ranging survey of techniques for reducing LLM inference cost including quantization, pruning, speculative decoding, batching, and hardware-aware serving.
summary: Covers the full stack of LLM inference optimisation: model compression (quantization, pruning, distillation), architectural efficiency (GQA, MoE, sliding-window attention), and system-level techniques (continuous batching, KV cache paging, speculative decoding). Provides benchmarks across TTFT and throughput metrics.
datacenter: Essential reading for AI infrastructure teams. Covers every lever available to improve tokens/s/GPU and reduce serving cost, directly mapping to cluster sizing, GPU generation selection, and software stack decisions.
metrics: Key result signal: end-to-end throughput and latency improvements versus baseline dense inference across serving stack optimisations.
